play_again: Do you want to play again?
exit_confirmation: Are you sure you want to quit?
objective_battle_1_1: Capture the flag to confirm the arrival of the British and notify the indicated post.
objective_battle_2_1: Steal the explosives from the British powder magazine and take them to the bridge.
objective_battle_4_1: Eliminate all British soldiers barricaded in the square.
battle_1_2:
In April 1806, British ships under the command of Colonel Popham
set sail from Cape Town with the aim of conquering Buenos Aires,
the most important trading hub in all of South America.

The long voyage began, and on May 20th, 1806, the first alarm
was raised among the authorities of the Viceroyalty.
battle_1_objective_3:
Capture the flag the British brought.
Then, report their arrival to the advance post
that will be indicated to you once the flag is captured.
battle_2_2:
After breaking through the defense put up by Viceroy Cisneros,
the British soldiers began their march with
the goal of conquering the fort of Buenos Aires: a strategic stronghold of
the Viceroyalty of the Río de la Plata. However, on the long road
separating them from their goal, they encountered a second obstacle:
"The Riachuelo". Near the present-day La Noria bridge, the British army
found this barrier standing in their way
before the conquest of Buenos Aires could become reality.
battle_2_objective_3:
Burn the bridge to stop the British from crossing.
To do so, steal explosives from the British Powder Magazine and
bring them to the bridge to set it on fire.
battle_3_2:
Even so, Beresford managed to cross the Riachuelo. Nothing could now stop the British advance
toward the capital of the Viceroyalty.
Completely demoralized, and following the sudden retreat of Sobremonte, the Spanish
officials awaited Beresford's arrival to surrender the city.

With the Fort already in General Beresford's hands, the British flag would soon
fly over Buenos Aires... and it would do so for 46 long days...
The people, outraged to see their city taken by a mere
handful of soldiers, would not take long to organize their own defense.
battle_3_objective_3:
Make your way from the bridge to the fort,
and recapture it.
battle_4_2:
The people, outraged to see their city taken by a mere
handful of soldiers, would not take long to organize their own defense.

Neighbors, criollos and Spaniards alike, rose up
against the British...

The people's resistance could only be overcome through reinforcements
that Beresford and Popham rushed to request from
the government in London. However, Santiago de Liniers, abandoning
his position in Ensenada, would catch the
British troops by surprise.
battle_4_objective_3: Destroy the British army in the square.
aftermath_1: Beresford signed the capitulation on August 20th, which included an agreement for the exchange of prisoners between both sides. With the city retaken, the Royal Audiencia of Buenos Aires assumed civil government and decided to hand the Captain Generalcy over to Liniers...
aftermath_2: Likewise, the Spanish Crown added the title "The Most Faithful and Reconquering" to the city of Montevideo, and fallen British flags were added to the city's coat of arms, marking the defeat of the British at Montevideo...
aftermath_3: Popham was tried by a British court-martial for having abandoned his mission at the Cape of Good Hope... but was only reprimanded. The city of London later awarded him an honorary sword for his efforts to open new markets; the sentence never truly affected him...
help_select_02:
Hold down the primary mouse button and drag to select
the area where your unit or units are located.
Remember you can only select a maximum
of 6 units at a time.
If you lose sight of your units on the map,
press U to cycle to the next unit.
help_move_02:
Select one or more units, then right-click
on the spot on the map where you want them to go.
help_attack_02:
With a unit or group selected, right-click
on the enemy unit you want to attack. When you can attack a unit,
the mouse cursor will change to a sword.
Whenever your troops spot an enemy unit, they will attack it
automatically.
help_objective_02:
The green arrow on screen shows the direction
of the current objective.
help_scroll_02:
You can move the camera to explore the terrain or
follow your troops by moving the mouse pointer
to the edges of the screen.
help_hud_02:
There is essential information in the interface that can help you:
Number of Argentine Units (A).
Number of British Units (B).
Status of the selected unit (C).
help_heal_02:
Select an injured unit and right-click
on an infirmary. Infirmaries are marked with a red cross.
help_tips_02: Don't forget to check the tips from time to time. There's a lot of useful information in them!
help_win_02:
To win battles you must complete the objectives
that appear on screen.
tip_01:
If you lost health, head to the buildings marked
with a red cross to recover.
tip_02:
There are several buildings where you can restore your health.
Look for them across the map.
tip_03: Surround the British and try to attack them by surprise.
tip_04:
Attack in coordinated groups —
you'll have a better chance of winning.
tip_05:
If you can't defeat them,
retreat and rethink your strategy.
tip_06:
Never attack an enemy that outnumbers you —
fall back and regroup your men.
tip_07:
Avoid unnecessary battles.
If you can, complete the objectives without killing any British soldiers.
tip_08:
To avoid being attacked, stay out of
the enemy's line of sight.
tip_09:
"Defend when you cannot defeat the enemy,
and attack when you can."
tip_10:
"The battlefield may seem like confusion and chaos,
but your own side must remain orderly.
That is what makes it proof against defeat."
tip_11:
"Move when it is advantageous and create changes in
the situation by dispersing and concentrating your forces."
tip_12:
"The most important thing in a military operation
is victory, not persistence."
tip_13: "It is better to keep an enemy intact than to destroy them."
tip_14:
"Defense is for times of scarcity,
attack is for times of abundance."
tip_15:
"Good strategists only win when it is easy to win...
Don't forget that."
tip_16:
"If you can attack a few soldiers with many,
you will whittle down the number of your adversaries."
tip_17:
Move the cursor to the edges of the screen
to navigate the map.
tip_18: Explore the map a little before moving your troops.
tip_19:
Don't forget you can find
your troops using the U key.
tip_20:
You can only manage groups of up to 6 units,
so plan your attacks by group carefully.
tip_21:
The green arrow on screen shows the direction
of the current objective. Don't forget it.
tip_22:
Don't waste time attacking unnecessarily.
Focus on the mission objectives.
tip_23:
If you get lost on the map, remember you can
find your troops again with the U key.
